facts.label: SMALLEST NATION EVER
facts.story: Iceland arrived in Russia with a population of roughly 345,000 — smaller than many World Cup stadiums fill across a season. Co-managed for years by Heimir Hallgrímsson, a dentist who worked part-time with the national team, the country had already stunned Europe by reaching the Euro 2016 quarter-finals. Qualifying for the 2018 World Cup made Iceland the smallest nation ever to reach the tournament, a record that stood until Curaçao matched the feat in 2026. Their qualifying run was near-perfect: six wins, four draws, zero losses.

facts.label: THE DIRECTOR WHO STOPPED MESSI
facts.story: Iceland goalkeeper Hannes Halldórsson moonlights as a film director — his credits include Iceland's 2012 Eurovision entry video. In the 63rd minute against Argentina, he dove right and parried a Lionel Messi penalty, having studied hours of footage the night before and noticing Messi favored the same corner every time. The save helped preserve a 1–1 draw in Iceland's World Cup debut and turned Halldórsson into an overnight global celebrity.

2018.headline: Smallest Nation, Biggest Stage

annotations.label: FIRST GOAL IN HISTORY
annotations.story: Four minutes after Sergio Agüero opened the scoring, Alfreð Finnbogason turned in a rebound to level it 1–1 against Argentina — the first goal Iceland had ever scored at a World Cup. It arrived in the team's very first match, on the biggest stage the tiny nation had ever played.

annotations.label: HALLDÓRSSON DENIES MESSI
annotations.story: In the 63rd minute, Lionel Messi stepped up to a penalty that could have put Argentina ahead. Goalkeeper Hannes Halldórsson, a part-time film director, guessed correctly and parried the shot away, preserving Iceland's 1–1 draw in the most famous save of the tournament's opening week.

annotations.label: PERIŠIĆ ENDS THE DREAM
annotations.story: Iceland's campaign ended in stoppage time. Milan Badelj's strike and a Gylfi Sigurðsson penalty had made it 1–1, but Ivan Perišić raced through and lashed home a 90th-minute winner for Croatia, closing the book on Iceland's fairy-tale debut with a 1–2 defeat.

2018.homeColors: blue shirt with a white collar and a red-white-blue dotted gradient on the sleeves (Errea)
2018.awayColors: white shirt with the same dotted sleeve graphic in blue tones (Errea)
